description.p: Unison is a file-synchronization tool for POSIX-compliant systems (e.g. *BSD, GNU/Linux, macOS) and Windows. It allows two replicas of a collection of files and directories to be stored on different hosts (or different disks on the same host), modified separately, and then brought up to date by propagating the changes in each replica to the other.
Note: if you want to connect to this Flatpak build remotely via SSH, put this in the profile file:

ul.li: Add ability to use reflinking instead of copying (if supported by filesystem).
Don't write file fingerprint cache if it is not necessary.
Experimental default-off support to detect moves/renames and rename on destination instead of copying (see -moves-experimental).

screenshot.caption: The main window, showing changed files
